title: Data Packing for SEQ Connector
text: Data Packing for SEQ Connector There are no field data types or packing codes in the same way as for a field-level DBMS—data is simply stored as entered. The connector creates and accesses sequential files only. It does not manage fields. When you create a table at run time, the connector creates a file to which everything is sequentially added. The connector handles the data in the file according to the conceptual definitions which you have already created. Records are stored in the same way as they are handled internally within Uniface. Default Storage Format for Certain Fields Some combinations of data type and packing code cause the SEQ connector to store data in a certain way. The table shows how Uniface packing codes are stored by the connector: Table 1. Storage of Uniface Packing Codes by Sequential File Connectors Uniface Sequential files D D3 ASCII date DDMMYYYY E E4 ASCII datetime DDMMYYYY HHNNSS F C20 T T1 ASCII time HH:NN:SS Was this topic helpful?
